test.note: Full test suite (go test ./...) timed out after 120s, but unit tests pass

blockingIssues.description: VAL-HEALTH-005 violation: Unknown service error returns fmt.Errorf() which gives exit code 1, but should give exit code 2 (USAGE_ERROR). The internal/errors package has UsageError type but it's not used here.
VAL-HEALTH-005 violation: checkConfluence() returns fmt.Errorf() for 'not configured' error which gives exit code 1, but should give exit code 3 (AUTH_ERROR).
VAL-HEALTH-005 violation: checkProtheus() returns fmt.Errorf() for 'no active profile' error which gives exit code 1, but should give exit code 3 (AUTH_ERROR).
VAL-HEALTH-005 violation: When Protheus ping fails with a network error (potentially retryable), the code returns fmt.Errorf() which gives exit code 1, but should give exit code 4 (RETRYABLE).
VAL-HEALTH-005 violation: checkTDS() 'no active profile' returns fmt.Errorf() instead of an error with exit code 3.
VAL-HEALTH-005 violation: TDS connection failure returns fmt.Errorf() which gives exit code 1, but network connection failures to AppServer could be retryable (exit code 4).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document when to use specialized error types (UsageError, AuthError, RetryableError) vs fmt.Errorf(). The internal/errors package provides these with proper exit codes but workers may not know to use them.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: health-check-command uses fmt.Errorf() exclusively, resulting in only exit codes 0 and 1, but the validation contract requires exit codes 2, 3, 4 for different error types.
